instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-type` that takes a place and a type designator. The expansion checks `(typep place type)` and signals a TYPE-ERROR (with `:datum` set to the place's value and `:expected-type` set to the type) when the check fails. Idiomatic CL pattern: an explicit, handler-case-able type-error is preferable to a plain ERROR for type assertions.
